name: Italy

authority.name: AgID — Agenzia per l'Italia Digitale (eIDAS supervisory body)
authority.operator: ~25 accredited QTSPs + Ministero dell'Interno (CIE national eID)

notes: Italy's qualified-signature trust is a federation of ~25 accredited QTSPs (Actalis, ArubaPEC, InfoCert, Namirial, Poste Italiane, Intesa, Intesi Group, TI Trust Technologies, Uanataca, and others) published in AgID's national eIDAS Trusted List. The 229 currently-granted CA certificates were promoted wholesale after verifying the Trusted List's XAdES signature through the EU LOTL chain of trust: the LOTL signature was checked against the pinned European Commission signing anchor, and the Italian TSL's signature (signer: AgID) against the identity the LOTL declares for Italy — so the list's single seal vouches for every certificate it contains (eIDAS Art. 22). Verification evidence is in VERIFICATION.md. In addition, the two Ministero dell'Interno / CNSD national eID roots behind the Carta d'Identità Elettronica (2016→2036, 2024→2044) are retained: they belong to the eID-notification mechanism rather than the QTSP list, and were cross-checked against the CIE portal and the AgID list independently.
